meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop spinning in tutorial hell. Learn how to use project management, Antigravity, and Claude Code to ship your SaaS MVP faster and drive real revenue.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a symptom of aimless coding. To escape, you must shift from 'learning' to 'shipping' by using professional project management. By combining Antigravity for architectural planning and Claude Code for automated execution, you convert abstract tutorials into a concrete, revenue-generating SaaS product with clear milestones.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'To-Do List App' tutorial, but your bank account remains at zero. You aren't building a product; you’re consuming content that promises progress but delivers stagnation. This cycle of endless imitation is the graveyard of potential SaaS empires, and it’s time to break the loop.

Real founders don't finish tutorials; they leverage them to solve specific business problems. By treating your development process as a disciplined project management exercise rather than a learning session, you force your code to serve the market. Let’s transform your scattered snippets into a high-converting MVP.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Stop coding features and start defining the core business value. We map your project requirements to a JSON structure to ensure the AI understands the business goal.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Based on my idea of [INSERT IDEA], create a lean MVP feature list prioritized by potential for user conversion. Output as a structured JSON object.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project-requirements.json file based on the Antigravity output.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Model
steps.description: Establish the database schema before writing a single line of logic. This prevents the 'refactor trap' that kills momentum.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Given the MVP feature list, design a normalized SQL schema. Include relationships for users, subscriptions, and core entities. Output as a Prisma-compatible schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate schema.prisma file and run migration.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Auth Layer
steps.description: Authentication is non-negotiable. We implement this early to secure the user journey and enable subscription gating.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure authentication flow using NextAuth.js, including email/password and social login providers, integrated with the Prisma schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Install NextAuth and configure the auth provider with existing database schema.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: We build one module at a time. This keeps focus high and prevents scope creep during the development phase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a task breakdown for the 'Dashboard' module. Focus on high-impact data visualization for the MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement dashboard module based on the provided task breakdown.'

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Loop
steps.description: Ship early, ship often. We automate the build process to ensure your progress is always visible to real users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel.

pro_tips: Never start a tutorial without a specific feature goal that aligns with your product roadmap.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; technical debt is the silent killer of MVPs.
If a tutorial doesn't teach you something that directly contributes to your monetization strategy, discard it.
Treat your commits like a business ledger; if it isn't documented in your project management tool, it didn't happen.
Prioritize 'ugly' functional code over 'perfect' architecture; you can refactor once you have paying users.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, stop adding features. Shift your focus entirely to user acquisition and feedback loops for the next 30 days.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint to stop watching and start building. The market doesn't care about your code quality as much as it cares about the value you provide. Stop the cycle, ship your MVP, and validate your business today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
